description: Golden fixtures pinning the distilled live-stream transform (R107). Each case maps one raw `log` SSE `data` line to the expected distilled output (`distilled: null` == suppress). See SPEC.md for the transform rules. The CLI reference implementation is roost.cli.distill_log_line; iOS (R108) and Android (R109) MUST produce identical output for every case.

cases.note: system/thinking_tokens -> suppressed (non-init system)
plain command stdout -> passthrough verbatim
roost internal event envelope -> passthrough (not stream-json)
malformed JSON-looking line -> passthrough verbatim

assistant envelope with truthy non-dict message (string) -> suppressed (R111). A truthy non-dict `message` is not a valid stream-json shape; all 3 platforms suppress it (Python isinstance(msg, dict), iOS as? [String:Any]->nil, Android optJSONObject->null) rather than calling .get()/[] on a non-dict and crashing the default distilled view.
tool_result with list content -> truncated result marker
result error -> failed phase divider
tool_use (WebSearch, query hint) -> arrow summary
tool_use with no hint key -> bare arrow
SPEC#1 JSON array at top level (not an object) -> passthrough verbatim
SPEC#1 JSON number at top level (not an object) -> passthrough verbatim
SPEC#1 leading-whitespace before a real envelope: trimmed for the {-test, parsed, distilled
SPEC#1 truncated/partial JSON (parse fails) -> passthrough verbatim (never lose a line)
SPEC#2 unknown top-level type -> passthrough verbatim (roost-internal never dropped)
SPEC#2 non-string `type` -> not a recognised envelope -> passthrough verbatim
SPEC#2 object with no `type` key -> passthrough verbatim
SPEC#3 result is_error truthy STRING ('yes') -> failed (JSON truthiness, R113 Android fix)
SPEC#3 result is_error number 1 (truthy) -> failed (R113: Android optBoolean was the outlier)
SPEC#3 result is_error number 0 (falsy) -> done
SPEC#3 result is_error empty-string (falsy) -> done
SPEC#4 assistant message is a LIST (truthy non-object) -> suppress (R111 class)
SPEC#4 user message is a NUMBER (truthy non-object) -> suppress (R111 class)
SPEC#4 content missing -> suppress
SPEC#4 content is a number (not string/list) -> suppress
SPEC#4 list content with non-object blocks (null/number/string/bool/list) ignored; only a text block survives
SPEC#4 text block with non-string `text` (number) -> empty -> block suppressed (R113 coercion pin)
SPEC#4 text block with null `text` -> empty -> suppressed (R113: was None/<null> on CLI/iOS)
SPEC#4 text block missing `text` -> empty -> suppressed
SPEC#4 text block whose value is only whitespace -> collapses to empty -> suppressed
SPEC#4 tool_use missing `name` -> literal 'tool'; string hint still rendered
SPEC#4 tool_use missing `input` -> bare arrow
SPEC#4 tool_use empty-string `name` -> literal 'tool'
SPEC#4 tool_use `input` not an object (a string) -> bare arrow (no hint)
SPEC#4 tool_use hint value is a NUMBER -> skipped -> bare arrow (R113: was '42' coercion)
SPEC#4 tool_use hint value is a BOOL -> skipped -> bare arrow (R113: True/true diverged)
SPEC#4 tool_use hint value is a LIST -> skipped -> bare arrow (R113: 3-way repr divergence)
SPEC#4 tool_use hint: empty-string `command` skipped, falls through to `file_path`
SPEC#4 tool_use hint: numeric `command` skipped, falls through to `file_path`
SPEC#4 tool_result content is a NUMBER (not string/list) -> '(result)' placeholder
SPEC#4 tool_result content is null -> '(result)'
SPEC#4 tool_result missing content -> '(result)'
SPEC#4 tool_result list content: number/null/non-text-object skipped; first text block wins
SPEC#4 tool_result list content: a leading non-empty string element is taken
SPEC#4 tool_result list text block with non-string `text` (number) skipped -> '(result)' (R113 Android optString coercion fix)
SPEC#4 tool_result is_error with empty content -> '✗ (result)'
SPEC#4 tool_result is_error truthy STRING ('yes') -> error marker (R113 Android fix)
SPEC#4 tool_result is_error number 1 (truthy) -> error marker (R113 Android fix)
SPEC#4 tool_result is_error number 0 (falsy) -> no error marker
SPEC#4 redacted_thinking block -> suppressed (drops the base64 blob, like thinking)
SPEC#4 user envelope: text block then tool_result joined with newline
SPEC#5 text exactly 199 chars (< 200 cap) -> verbatim, no ellipsis
SPEC#5 text exactly 200 chars (== cap) -> verbatim, no ellipsis (boundary)
SPEC#5 text 201 chars (> cap) -> first 200 + single ellipsis
SPEC#5 hint exactly 79 chars (< 80 cap) -> verbatim, no ellipsis
SPEC#5 hint exactly 80 chars (== cap) -> verbatim, no ellipsis (boundary)
SPEC#5 hint 81 chars (> cap) -> first 80 + single ellipsis
SPEC#5 unicode (accents, BMP symbol, astral emoji) preserved through collapse
SPEC#5 control chars (tab/newline/CR) collapse like whitespace into single spaces
